questions.question: What is a Queue data structure?
options: A) A collection of elements that are ordered in a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) manner.
B) A set of elements that can be accessed in any order.
C) A list of elements that are ordered in a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) manner.
D) A map of elements that are ordered by their keys.
questions.explanation: A Queue is a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure, meaning the first element that is added to the queue will be the first one to be removed.

questions.question: What is the primary operation in a Queue data structure?
options: A) Insertion at both ends
B) Deletion at both ends
C) Insertion at one end and deletion at the other end
D) Deletion at the front of the queue and insertion at the rear of the queue.
questions.explanation: In a Queue, the primary operation is Deletion at the front of the queue and insertion at the rear of the queue.

questions.question: How can a Queue be implemented using an array?
options: A) Using a single array
B) Using two arrays one for front and one for rear
C) Using a linked list
D) Using a stack.
questions.explanation: A Queue can be implemented using a linked list or two arrays, one for the front and one for the rear of the queue.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a Circular Queue and a Standard Queue?
options: A) A Circular Queue is a linear data structure while a Standard Queue is a tree-based data structure.
B) A Circular Queue has a fixed size while a Standard Queue does not.
C) A Circular Queue is used for tasks that require a fixed size while a Standard Queue is used for tasks that do not.
D) A Circular Queue is used for tasks that require a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) order while a Standard Queue is used for tasks that require a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) order.
questions.explanation: A Circular Queue is used for tasks that require a fixed size and a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) order, whereas a Standard Queue is used for tasks that do not require a fixed size and can use either First-In-First-Out (FIFO) or Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) order.

questions.question: What type of Queue allows insertion and deletion at both ends?
options: A) Priority Queue
B) Circular Queue
C) Double-Ended Queue
D) Standard Queue.
questions.explanation: A Double-Ended Queue is a type of Queue that allows insertion and deletion at both ends.
